knowledge_base: Product CGI Prompt Engineering - Master Formula

statements: The fundamental structure for product CGI prompts is: [Product type] in [setting or environment], [camera movement] with [angle], [lighting and mood], [style reference], cinematic, high detail, realistic CGI, product-focused

Example application: Luxury perfume bottle on marble pedestal, camera slowly rotates around product with dramatic side lighting, close-up shot, moody shadows, golden hour style, hyperreal CGI, inspired by Apple ads

Master prompt example: Matte black wireless earbuds floating above a stone pedestal, camera orbiting 360 degrees, dramatic spotlight casting soft shadow on white background, macro angle reveals brand logo, cinematic Apple-style CGI, moody and modern
Orbit / Rotate: Use 'camera rotates 360° around the product' to showcase all angles of the product from a complete circular perspective
Push-in: Use 'slow push-in toward the product' to highlight details and packaging with progressive focus intensification
Pull-out: Use 'camera pulls away to reveal full...' for dramatic reveals and smooth transitions from close-up to wide view
Crane up/down: Use 'crane up from bottom to top of product' to emphasize height or vertical elements of the product
Tilt down/up: Use 'camera tilts down over the product' to reveal branding or unique features on the top surface
Top-down pan: Use 'overhead camera pans slowly across product' for flat-lay or luxury overview presentations
Slide / Track: Use 'camera slides sideways past product' for tech-style sleek motion and profile reveals
Zoom in/out: Use 'camera zooms into product label' to draw focus to logos, text, and fine details
Rotation-in-place: Use 'product rotates while camera is static' for floating or spotlight-focused looks
POV reveal: Use 'POV reaching to grab the product' for lifestyle integration and immersive perspective

entries.category: Camera Angles to Pair with CGI Products
statements: Close-up: Use 'close-up on product texture' to show materials, texture details, and branding elements with magnified clarity
Macro: Use 'extreme close-up of surface' for skin creams, tech finishes, and small details requiring extreme magnification
Medium angle: Use 'eye-level view of product' for balanced, standard advertising looks that feel natural and accessible
Low angle: Use 'low angle to emphasize power' for luxury, bold packaging that conveys strength and prestige
Top-down / Flatlay: Use 'flat lay shot from overhead' for beauty products, packaging reveals, and minimalist compositions
Dutch angle: Use 'tilted angle for creative style' for edgy fashion or conceptual shots requiring visual tension
Backlit silhouette: Use 'backlight with rim light edge' for elegant reveals, perfume bottles, and watches with luminous edges
Floating in Light Box: Product floating mid-air with camera orbiting, spotlight highlights, and black backdrop for dramatic isolation
Cinematic Pouring Effect: Liquid (oil, perfume) pouring in slow-motion with macro angle for viscous, luxurious movement capture
Futuristic Turntable Reveal: Product on rotating base with neon background and reflections on surface for high-tech aesthetic
Nature vs Tech Contrast: Camera slides across product in moss/forest setting versus sleek backdrop for conceptual juxtaposition
Luxury Spa Vibe: Steaming water, marble tiles, smooth tracking over bottle with candlelight mood for premium wellness positioning
Use 'backlight' for rim-lit silhouettes and edge definition on translucent or reflective products
Use 'soft shadows' for gentle, diffused shadow transitions that enhance form without harsh contrast
Use 'moody lighting' for atmospheric, emotionally evocative illumination with selective highlights
Use 'rim light' for edge-defining light that separates product from background with luminous outlines
Use 'studio spot' for focused, directional light creating dramatic highlights and controlled shadow play
Use 'dramatic contrast' for high-key/low-key lighting ratios that emphasize product dimensionality
Use 'reflective surface' for shiny, mirror-like finishes that capture and bounce light realistically
Use 'chrome' for polished metal with high specularity and mirror-like reflections
Use 'matte black' for non-reflective, absorptive surfaces with deep color saturation
Use 'glassy' for transparent or semi-transparent materials with refractive properties
Use 'brushed aluminum' for directional surface finish with parallel micro-scratches and soft reflections
Use 'velvet' for soft, light-absorbing fabric with minimal specularity and rich color depth
Use 'marble' for natural stone with veining, translucency, and subtle reflective properties
Use 'water droplets' for realistic liquid beads on surfaces with refraction and reflection
Use 'glowing edges' for luminescent rim lighting or self-emissive surface properties
Use 'soft mist' for atmospheric volumetric fog or haze creating depth and mood
Use 'particles in light beam' for visible dust or particle effects illuminated by directional light
Use 'steam' for volumetric vapor effects suggesting warmth or luxury spa environments
Use 'shimmer' for subtle, shimmering surface reflections and light-catching movement
Use 'product photography' for professional, polished aesthetic with controlled lighting and composition
Use 'luxury packaging' for premium visual treatment emphasizing exclusivity and high-end positioning
Use 'hyperreal' for photorealistic rendering with meticulous detail and material accuracy
Use 'cinematic CGI' for film-quality production with dramatic lighting and professional color grading
Use 'Apple-style ad' for minimalist, elegant aesthetic with focus on form and premium presentation
Use 'white studio backdrop' for clean, professional isolation allowing product to be the sole focus
Use 'black void' for dramatic, high-contrast isolation with luminous product definition
Use 'nature-inspired set' for organic, environmental context suggesting natural positioning
Use 'modern shelf' for contemporary retail or display context with minimalist staging
Use 'floating scene' for weightless, surreal environment suggesting premium, ethereal positioning
Pan left/right: Use 'camera pans left' or 'camera pans right' for horizontal sweeping motion across the product
Tilt up/down: Use 'camera tilts up' or 'camera tilts down' for vertical sweeping motion revealing vertical product elements
Dolly in/out: Use 'camera dollies in slowly' or 'camera dollies out dramatically' for forward/backward movement with perspective shift
Crane up/down: Use 'camera cranes up from ground level' or 'camera cranes down from sky view' for elevated perspective changes
Push-in: Use 'camera pushes in slowly toward subject's face' for progressive focus intensification toward product details
Pull-out: Use 'camera pulls out to reveal landscape' for context expansion from close-up to environmental view
Tracking shot: Use 'camera tracks behind the subject walking' for following motion parallel to product movement
Orbit shot: Use 'camera orbits around the subject in 360' for complete circular perspective of stationary product
Handheld: Use 'handheld camera shakes gently, raw documentary style' for organic, naturalistic movement
POV: Use 'POV shot from character's eyes' for first-person immersive product interaction perspective
Zoom in/out: Use 'zoom in on expression' or 'zoom out to wide view' for optical magnification without physical camera movement
Dutch angle: Use 'slight dutch angle for unease' for tilted frame creating visual tension or creative dynamism
Static shot: Use 'static wide angle shot' for motionless camera capturing complete environmental context
Steadicam: Use 'smooth steadicam following subject' for fluid, stabilized motion following product movement
Time-lapse: Use 'time-lapse of sunset over cityscape' for compressed temporal progression showing environmental changes
Slow motion: Use 'slow motion raindrops hitting pavement' for extended temporal expansion emphasizing motion details
Rack focus: Use 'camera rack focuses from flower to face' for selective focus transition between foreground and subject
Over-the-shoulder: Use 'over-the-shoulder angle watching crowd' for secondary perspective viewing product interaction
Aerial shot: Use 'aerial drone shot of desert landscape' for elevated bird's-eye environmental perspective
Wide tracking: Use 'wide shot, camera tracks sideways with subject' for lateral movement maintaining wide environmental context
Low angle: Use 'from low angle looking up' to emphasize product height, power, and imposing presence
High angle: Use 'from high angle looking down' to emphasize product scale, context, and environmental relationship
Eye-level: Use 'at eye level' for natural, comfortable viewing perspective matching human visual experience
Overhead: Use 'top-down overhead view' for flat-lay, packaging reveal, and minimalist composition
Worm's eye view: Use 'extreme low angle' for dramatic upward perspective emphasizing product dominance
Bird's eye view: Use 'bird's eye perspective' for complete overhead context showing product in environment
Dutch angle: Use 'off-kilter dutch angle' for tilted, dynamic composition creating visual interest and tension
Close-up: Use 'tight close-up on expression' for magnified detail focus on product features and textures
Medium shot: Use 'medium shot from side' for balanced product visibility with contextual environmental elements
Wide shot: Use 'wide establishing shot' for full environmental context with product as focal point
Extreme wide shot: Use 'vast landscape with subject small' for environmental emphasis with product as contextual element

entries.category: Keywords for Realism & Style - Lighting & Weather
statements: Use 'overcast' for soft, diffused natural lighting without harsh shadows or direct sunlight
Use 'harsh shadows' for high-contrast, dramatic shadow definition emphasizing form and dimensionality
Use 'soft lighting' for gentle, diffused illumination minimizing shadow intensity and contrast
Use 'golden hour' for warm, directional light during sunrise/sunset creating romantic, premium atmosphere
Use 'blue hour' for cool, twilight illumination creating moody, sophisticated ambiance
Use 'flickering neon' for dynamic, colorful artificial light creating contemporary, urban aesthetic
Use 'moody backlight' for atmospheric rim lighting creating silhouettes and luminous edges
Use 'heavy snowfall' for volumetric precipitation creating atmospheric depth and seasonal context
Use 'foggy morning' for volumetric mist creating ethereal, mysterious atmosphere
Use 'dusty sunset' for warm, particle-filled light creating atmospheric depth and warmth

entries.category: Keywords for Realism & Style - Cinematic Styles
statements: Use '35mm film look' for warm color grading and organic film grain aesthetic
Use 'analog grain' for visible film grain texture suggesting vintage, organic quality
Use 'cinematic LUT' for professional color grading matching cinema production standards
Use 'inspired by Blade Runner' for neo-noir aesthetic with cool tones and dramatic lighting
Use 'Wong Kar Wai aesthetic' for saturated colors, neon lighting, and romantic visual composition
Use 'vintage lens distortion' for optical aberrations and vignetting suggesting classic optics
Use 'bokeh background' for blurred background with smooth, circular out-of-focus highlights

entries.category: Keywords for Realism & Style - Motion Keywords
statements: Use 'slow' for extended temporal duration emphasizing deliberate, controlled movement
Use 'sweeping' for broad, expansive camera motion covering large spatial areas
Use 'shaky' for unstable, handheld camera movement suggesting organic, naturalistic capture
Use 'fluid' for smooth, continuous motion without acceleration or deceleration
Use 'jittery' for rapid, irregular micro-movements suggesting nervous or energetic energy
Use 'robotic' for precise, mechanical motion suggesting controlled, automated movement
Use 'dreamlike' for slow, ethereal motion suggesting surreal, non-linear temporal progression
Use 'dynamic' for energetic, varied motion suggesting active, engaging visual experience

entries.category: Keywords for Realism & Style - Mood Keywords
statements: Use 'eerie' for unsettling, mysterious atmosphere suggesting tension or supernatural elements
Use 'nostalgic' for emotionally evocative atmosphere suggesting memory, history, or vintage quality
Use 'tense' for dramatic, suspenseful atmosphere suggesting conflict or anticipation
Use 'peaceful' for calm, serene atmosphere suggesting tranquility and relaxation
Use 'hopeful' for optimistic, uplifting atmosphere suggesting possibility and positive emotion
Use 'melancholic' for emotionally somber atmosphere suggesting sadness or introspection
Use 'gritty' for raw, textured atmosphere suggesting authenticity and industrial aesthetic
Use 'surreal' for dreamlike, impossible atmosphere suggesting fantasy or non-realistic elements
